title: Impact of everolimus-related interstitial lung disease on subsequent treatment in patients with metastatic breast cancer.
abstract: Everolimus is an option for second-line or later use in combination with endocrine therapy for hormone receptor-positive breast cancer. An important everolimus-associated adverse event is drug-related interstitial lung disease (ILD), reported to occur in 16.0% of individuals in the BOLERO-2 trial, and 23.5% of those in the Asian subgroup. We aimed to examine the impact of everolimus-related ILD (e-rILD) on the risk of drug-related ILD during subsequent anti-cancer treatment.
